scope.summary: Expose current_schema and service_name only to stdio and server-observed loopback HTTP connection-info calls, retaining the full remote HTTP redaction set.
out_of_scope: Changing the remote HTTP redaction policy for any other connection identity or topology field.
Trusting caller-supplied forwarding headers to classify a remote peer as local.

entry_points.name: oracle_connection_info and current_database over stdio or loopback HTTP
entry_points.trace: connection_info_reports_the_active_profile asserts the local response exposes current_schema and service_name; stateful_http_lanes_keep_profile_switches_and_connections_isolated exercises the real loopback HTTP route and asserts its current schema is present while session_user, module, and action remain redacted.

entry_points.name: oracle_connection_info over remote HTTP
entry_points.trace: connection_info_keeps_schema_and_service_redacted_for_remote_transport sets the server-observed transport flag false and asserts both fields are omitted and still listed in redacted_fields.

limitations: Scoped checks only: cargo test -p oraclemcp connection_info_ (5 passed), cargo test -p oraclemcp --test e2e_http_oauth stateful_http_lanes_keep_profile_switches_and_connections_isolated (1 passed), and cargo test -p oraclemcp-core owned_dispatch_context_preserves_lane_time_and_caller_budget (1 passed). The full workspace gate was not run for this close.
The remote-policy test exercises the trusted DispatchContext boundary directly; no non-loopback listener was started for this close.
